column2: Why should a technician avoid describing a hard drive or SSD as RAM?
column4: RAM is working memory used while software is running; a hard drive or SSD is long-term storage that keeps data after power is removed.
column5:
Common Confusion

Both RAM and storage affect system performance, but they solve different problems. RAM holds active data for the CPU, while drives provide persistent storage.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.30; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column10: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Basic RAM Storage Source::Messer-v170

column2: A laptop needs a memory replacement. Which RAM form factor is the likely match: DIMM or SODIMM?
column4: SODIMM is the likely match because it is the smaller RAM module form factor commonly used in laptops and compact systems.
column5:
Exam Trap

DIMM and SODIMM are both memory modules, but physical size and slot type matter. A desktop-style DIMM will not fit a SODIMM slot.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.30; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column10: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Basic HighYield RAM FormFactors Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A motherboard supports DDR4 memory. Why is DDR5 not a safe replacement choice even if the capacity looks acceptable?
column4: DDR generations are not backward-compatible; the motherboard, slot keying, and supported memory generation must match.
column5:
Why

Capacity is only one RAM characteristic. A replacement module must match the platform's supported DDR generation, not just the desired number of gigabytes.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.30-31; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column10: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Basic HighYield RAM DDR Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which RAM type is more appropriate for a critical server that should detect and correct certain memory errors: ECC or non-ECC?
column4: ECC RAM is more appropriate because it can detect and correct certain memory errors while the system is running.
column5:
Exam Trap

ECC is associated with reliability-focused systems such as servers. Non-ECC memory is common in consumer systems but does not provide the same correction capability.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.31; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column10: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Basic HighYield RAM ECC Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Before installing ECC RAM, what compatibility concern should a technician verify?
column4: Verify that the system or platform supports ECC and that the installed modules match that requirement.
column5:
Why

ECC is not only a module label. If the platform does not support ECC behavior, buying ECC modules does not guarantee error-correction support.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.31; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column10: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Basic RAM ECC Compatibility Source::Messer-v170

column2: What is the purpose of a multi-channel RAM configuration such as dual-channel memory?
column4: It increases CPU-to-RAM throughput by using more than one memory channel.
column5:
Why

The channel count describes memory paths, not the amount of RAM by itself. Two modules installed correctly can provide more memory bandwidth than one comparable module.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.31; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column10: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Basic HighYield RAM Channels Source::Messer-v170

column2: A system should use dual-channel memory. What installation habit best supports that goal?
column4: Install closely matched memory modules in the motherboard slots specified for the channel configuration.
column5:
Why

Channel mode depends on the motherboard and memory controller. Matching modules and using the correct slots give the system the best chance of using the intended channel configuration.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.31; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.3
column10: A+::220-1201::3.3 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::RAMCharacteristics Basic HighYield RAM Channels Scenario Source::Messer-v170
